question: How many dockets have a short case name starting with 'S'?
Which courts have the most dockets with 'United States v.' in their case name?
gold_sparql:
SELECT ?court_name (COUNT(?docket) AS ?docket_count) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets> WHERE {
  ?docket <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/type/Docket> .
  ?docket <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/docket_filed_in_court> ?court .
  ?court <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/type/Court> .
  ?court <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/court_full_name> ?court_name .
  ?docket <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/docket_case_name> ?case_name .
  FILTER (CONTAINS(?case_name, "United States v."))
} GROUP BY ?court_name ORDER BY DESC(?docket_count) LIMIT 10
expected_answer:

District Court, D. Wyoming | 38

District Court, D. South Dakota | 28

per_seed.answer:

per_seed.code:
import pandas as pd

dockets = dfs['Docket']
courts = dfs['Court']

# Filter dockets for 'United States v.' in the case name
us_v_dockets = dockets[dockets['docket_case_name'].str.contains('United States v.', na=False, case=False)]

# Count dockets per court_id
court_counts = us_v_dockets['docket_court_id'].value_counts().reset_index()
court_counts.columns = ['court_id', 'count']

# Merge with Court table to get the court names
merged = pd.merge(court_counts, courts, on='court_id')

# Find the maximum count
max_count = merged['count'].max()

# Get all courts that have this maximum count
top_courts = merged[merged['count'] == max_count]['court_full_name'].unique().tolist()

result = top_courts

import pandas as pd

# Merge Docket with Judge to get the judge-court relationship
# Note: docket_judge_assigned_id is the link to Judge table
dockets = dfs['Docket']
judges = dfs['Judge']

# Filter out dockets without an assigned judge
dockets_with_judges = dockets[dockets['docket_judge_assigned_id'].notna()]

# Count dockets per judge
judge_counts = dockets_with_judges.groupby('docket_judge_assigned_id').size().reset_index(name='docket_count')

# Merge with Judge table to get the court_id for each judge
judge_court_counts = pd.merge(judge_counts, judges, left_on='docket_judge_assigned_id', right_on='judge_id')

# Group by court and calculate the mean of docket counts per judge
court_avg_dockets = judge_court_counts.groupby('judge_court_id')['docket_count'].mean().reset_index()

# Format result as a list of [court_id, average]
result = court_avg_dockets.values.tolist()

question: For each court, what is the average number of dockets that have a 'Civil Miscellaneous Case' cause, grouped by the year they were filed?
gold_sparql:
SELECT ?courtName ?yearValue (COUNT(?docket) AS ?numberOfDockets) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets> WHERE {
  ?court <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/type/Court> .
  ?court <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/court_full_name> ?courtName .
  ?docket <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/type/Docket> .
  ?docket <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/docket_filed_in_court> ?court .
  ?docket <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/docket_cause> "Civil Miscellaneous Case" .
  ?docket <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/docket_filed_in_year> ?year .
  ?year <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/type/Year> .
  ?year <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/year_value> ?yearValue .
} GROUP BY ?courtName ?yearValue
ORDER BY ?courtName ?yearValue

import pandas as pd

# Filter dockets for 'Civil Miscellaneous Case' cause
filtered_dockets = dfs['Docket'][dfs['Docket']['docket_cause'] == 'Civil Miscellaneous Case'].copy()

# Extract year from docket_date_filed
filtered_dockets['year_filed'] = pd.to_datetime(filtered_dockets['docket_date_filed']).dt.year

# Group by court and year to get counts
counts = filtered_dockets.groupby(['docket_court_id', 'year_filed']).size().reset_index(name='docket_count')

# Calculate average number of dockets per year for each court
avg_dockets = counts.groupby('docket_court_id')['docket_count'].mean().reset_index()

# Merge with Court table to get the court names
merged = pd.merge(avg_dockets, dfs['Court'], left_on='docket_court_id', right_on='court_id')

# Format result as a list of [court_full_name, average_count]
result = merged[['court_full_name', 'docket_count']].values.tolist()

SELECT ?judgeName (COUNT(DISTINCT ?docket) AS ?docketCount) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets> WHERE { 
  ?judge <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/type/Judge> .
  ?judge <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/judge_name> ?judgeName .
  ?docket <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/type/Docket> .
  ?docket <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/docket_assigned_to_judge> ?judge .
  ?docket <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/docket_filed_in_court> ?court .
  ?court <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/type/Court> .
  ?court <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-pacer-federal-dockets/pred/court_full_name> ?courtFullName .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?courtFullName)), "district"))
} GROUP BY ?judgeName
ORDER BY DESC(?docketCount)

import pandas as pd

# Filter courts with 'District' in their full name
district_courts = dfs['Court'][dfs['Court']['court_full_name'].str.contains('District', case=False, na=False)]

# Merge dockets with these courts
dockets_in_district_courts = pd.merge(dfs['Docket'], district_courts, left_on='docket_court_id', right_on='court_id')

# Merge with judges to get judge names
dockets_with_judges = pd.merge(dockets_in_district_courts, dfs['Judge'], left_on='docket_judge_assigned_id', right_on='judge_id')

# Group by judge name and count dockets
judge_counts = dockets_with_judges.groupby('judge_name').size().reset_index(name='count')

# Convert to list of [judge_name, count]
result = judge_counts.values.tolist()

import pandas as pd

# Filter courts with 'District' in their full name
district_courts = dfs['Court'][dfs['Court']['court_full_name'].str.contains('District', case=False, na=False)]

# Merge Dockets with these courts
dockets_in_district_courts = pd.merge(dfs['Docket'], district_courts, left_on='docket_court_id', right_on='court_id')

# Merge with Judge to get judge names
dockets_with_judges = pd.merge(dockets_in_district_courts, dfs['Judge'], left_on='docket_judge_assigned_id', right_on='judge_id')

# Group by judge name and count dockets
judge_counts = dockets_with_judges.groupby('judge_name').size().reset_index(name='count')

# Convert to list of [name, count]
result = judge_counts.values.tolist()
